# System-Level Tools (bundled in /bin or installed on host PC)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# These are external binaries the Python scripts call via subprocess.
# All should be bundled inside the .exe package where possible,
# or verified at startup with clear user-facing error if missing.

# ── ANDROID ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
Android Platform Tools (ADB)
  Version: 37.0.0
  Source:  https://developer.android.com/tools/releases/platform-tools
  Location: /bin/adb/                                  ← pre-built, ready to use
  Key files: adb.exe, AdbWinApi.dll, AdbWinUsbApi.dll, libwinpthread-1.dll
  Usage:   All Android device communication, file transfer, shell commands

# ── iOS — LIBIMOBILEDEVICE BINARY LAYER ──────────────────────
# All EXEs and DLLs were obtained as pre-built binaries via MSYS2 pacman
# (MinGW64 toolchain). They are now in /bin/libimobiledevice/ and ready
# to bundle — NO compilation required.
#
# pymobiledevice3 (Python) is the PRIMARY iOS layer — these are FALLBACK only.
# Vendor source repos in /vendor/ are retained as reference and in case a
# specific version or custom build is ever needed.
#
# MSYS2 packages used:
#   pacman -S mingw-w64-x86_64-libimobiledevice       → 1.3.0
#   pacman -S mingw-w64-x86_64-ideviceinstaller        → 1.1.1
#   pacman -S mingw-w64-x86_64-libplist                → 2.7.0
#   pacman -S mingw-w64-x86_64-libusbmuxd              → 2.1.1
#   pacman -S mingw-w64-x86_64-libimobiledevice-glue   → 1.3.2
#   pacman -S mingw-w64-x86_64-libzip                  → 1.11.4
#   pacman -S mingw-w64-x86_64-openssl                 → 3.6.1
#   pacman -S mingw-w64-x86_64-ntldd-git               → (dep audit tool, dev only)

#  [BUILT] libimobiledevice  v1.3.0  (vendor has 1.4.0 — 1.3.0 adequate for fallback)
  Location: /bin/libimobiledevice/                     ← pre-built, ready to bundle
  DLL: libimobiledevice-1.0.dll
  Vendor source: /vendor/libimobiledevice-1.4.0/       ← if 1.4.0 ever specifically needed

#  [BUILT] libplist  v2.7.0
  Location: /bin/libimobiledevice/
  DLL: libplist-2.0.dll
  Vendor source: /vendor/libplist-2.7.0/

#  [BUILT] libusbmuxd  v2.1.1
  Location: /bin/libimobiledevice/
  DLL: libusbmuxd-2.0.dll
  Vendor source: /vendor/libusbmuxd-2.1.1/

#  [BUILT] libimobiledevice-glue  v1.3.2
  Location: /bin/libimobiledevice/
  DLL: libimobiledevice-glue-1.0.dll
  Vendor source: /vendor/libimobiledevice-glue-1.3.2/

#  [BUILT] ideviceinstaller  v1.1.1  (vendor has 1.2.0 — 1.1.1 adequate)
  Location: /bin/libimobiledevice/
  EXE: ideviceinstaller.exe
  Vendor source: /vendor/ideviceinstaller-1.2.0/

# ── CONFIRMED DLL MANIFEST (verified via ntldd -R) ───────────
# These are the ONLY non-Windows DLLs required by the idevice tools.
# MSYS2 packages were compiled with -static-libgcc / -static-libstdc++
# so NO libgcc_s_seh-1.dll or libwinpthread-1.dll is needed at runtime.

# Core (required by ALL idevice EXEs):
#   libimobiledevice-1.0.dll
#   libssl-3-x64.dll
#   libcrypto-3-x64.dll
#   libplist-2.0.dll
#   libusbmuxd-2.0.dll
#   libimobiledevice-glue-1.0.dll

# ideviceinstaller extras (required only by ideviceinstaller.exe):
#   libzip.dll
#   libbz2-1.dll
#   liblzma-5.dll
#   zlib1.dll
#   libzstd.dll

# OpenSSL legacy cipher module (for older iOS pairing/backup crypto):
#   ossl-modules/legacy.dll   ← MUST be in ossl-modules/ subfolder relative to EXEs

# Total: 11 DLLs + 1 subfolder DLL + 23 EXEs = 35 entries in /bin/libimobiledevice/

# ── IDEVICE TOOLS INCLUDED ───────────────────────────────────
# idevice_id           — list/identify connected devices
# idevicebackup        — legacy MobileSync backup (iOS ≤ 12)
# idevicebackup2       — full backup/restore via MobileSync
# idevicecrashreport   — pull crash logs from device
# idevicedate          — get/set device clock
# idevicedebug         — attach debugserver
# idevicedebugserverproxy — proxy debugserver over TCP
# idevicediagnostics   — device diagnostics / reboot / shutdown
# ideviceenterrecovery — put device into recovery mode
# ideviceimagemounter  — mount developer disk image
# ideviceinfo          — query lockdown info / device properties
# ideviceinstaller     — install/remove/list apps (IPA sideload)
# idevicename          — get/set device name
# idevicenotificationproxy — send/receive notifications
# idevicepair          — pair, validate, unpair device
# ideviceprovision     — manage provisioning profiles
# idevicescreenshot    — capture device screenshot
# idevicesetlocation   — GPS spoof (developer mode)
# idevicesyslog        — stream device syslog
# inetcat              — TCP tunnel via usbmuxd
# iproxy               — port forward over USB
# plistutil            — plist binary/XML inspection (dev utility)
# openssl              — cert/key inspection (dev utility)

#  [MAYBE] libirecovery  v1.3.1
  Source:   https://github.com/libimobiledevice/libirecovery
  Location: /vendor/libirecovery-1.3.1/                ← source, held unbuilt
  Note:     NOT available as MSYS2 package — would need manual compile from vendor.
  Output if activated: libirecovery-1.0.dll, irecovery.exe
  Usage:    DFU and recovery mode communication.
  Decision: Do NOT compile unless recovery mode handling is explicitly scoped in.

#  [NO] usbmuxd (the daemon)
  Reason:   On Windows, Apple provides this via AppleMobileDeviceService.exe
            (installed with iTunes or Apple Devices app from Microsoft Store).
            Installing the OSS daemon would conflict with Apple's service.

#  [NO] ifuse
  Reason:   FUSE filesystem mount — Linux/macOS only. No Windows support.

#  [NO] idevicerestore
  Reason:   Full firmware/IPSW restore. Completely outside project scope.

#  [NO] libtatsu
  Reason:   Apple TSS server / firmware signing. Out of scope.

#  [NO] ideviceactivation
  Reason:   Device activation protocol. Out of scope.

#  [NO] libgpod
  Reason:   Legacy iPod media library. Irrelevant.

# ── COMPILE ORDER (if vendor source ever needed) ─────────────
# Only needed if MSYS2 packages are insufficient (wrong version, missing feature).
# Build via MSYS2 MinGW64 shell with MinGW-w64 toolchain:
#   1. libplist-2.7.0
#   2. libimobiledevice-glue-1.3.2  (depends on libplist)
#   3. libusbmuxd-2.1.1             (depends on libplist)
#   4. libimobiledevice-1.4.0       (depends on all three above)
#   5. ideviceinstaller-1.2.0       (depends on libimobiledevice + libplist)

# ── MEDIA PROCESSING ─────────────────────────────────────────
FFmpeg
  Version: 8.0.1 full build
  Source:  https://www.gyan.dev/ffmpeg/builds/ (Windows static builds)
  Location: /bin/ffmpeg/                               ← pre-built, ready to use
  Key files: /bin/ffmpeg/bin/ffmpeg.exe, ffplay.exe, ffprobe.exe
  Usage:
    - OGG → AAC/MP3 audio conversion
    - MOV → MP4 container remux (no re-encode)
    - M4A → MP3 for Android compatibility
    - M4R ringtone creation (AAC → M4R, 30s cap)
    - CAF (iOS audio format) → M4A
    - General audio normalization

libheif  (via pillow-heif Python binding)
  Version: 1.17+
  Source:  Installed as part of pillow-heif pip package
  Bundle:  Handled by pip — Python wheel includes the native binary
  Usage:   HEIC/HEIF image decoding → JPEG for Android destinations

# ── RUNTIME ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
Python 3.11+
  Used for: All PC-side script execution
  Bundled:  YES when building .exe via PyInstaller (self-contained)
  Standalone use: User must have Python 3.11+ if running scripts directly

Visual C++ Redistributable (vc_redist)
  Version: 2015-2022 (x64, x86, arm64)
  Source:  Microsoft — binaries in /bin/redist/
  Bundle:  YES — silently run VC_redist.x64.exe at first launch before any other step
  Usage:   Required by ADB binaries on Windows. NOT present on a fresh Windows install.
  Note:    libimobiledevice DLLs do NOT require vc_redist — MSYS2 packages
           statically link their C/C++ runtimes (-static-libgcc).

# ── SYSTEM REQUIREMENTS ───────────────────────────────────────────
iTunes (Apple website)
  Required: YES — provides AppleMobileDeviceService.exe and Apple USB drivers
  Bundle:   YES — installer in /bin/redist/iTunes64Setup.exe
  Detection: Check for AppleMobileDeviceService in Windows services at startup.
             If missing, launch iTunes64Setup.exe automatically (shows iTunes UI wizard).
  Note:     Using the standalone Apple website installer, not the Microsoft Store
            version. Both provide the same driver stack.

Apple Application Support (32-bit and 64-bit)
  Required: Installed automatically alongside iTunes / Apple Devices
  Usage:    Required for AFC/usbmuxd named pipe communication on Windows

Android USB Drivers
  Required (baseline): Google USB Driver only (android_winusb.inf).
                       This is the single ADB interface driver that
                       covers virtually every modern Android device in
                       ADB mode — it binds via WinUSB on Windows 10/11
                       once USB debugging is enabled and the user
                       accepts the "Trust this computer" prompt.
                       Matches core.prerequisite_checker._ANDROID_DRIVER_REQUIRED.

  Optional (fallbacks): The 13 OEM driver installers in /bin/drivers/android/
                       (sourced from Chimera's driver pack, ~202 MB total)
                       are NOT required by ADB and are NOT required by
                       PhoneTransfer.  They cover OEM-specific service /
                       diagnostic / download protocols (Qualcomm 9008/EDL,
                       MediaTek BROM, Samsung Odin, vendor service modes)
                       which PhoneTransfer never invokes — its entire
                       Android-side communication path is `adb.exe` over
                       USB or Wi-Fi, plus the companion APK over an
                       ADB-forwarded TCP socket.
                       Keep them around as a fallback for the rare device
                       Google's generic driver doesn't recognise (typically
                       very old Android-4-era hardware), but they should
                       NOT be installed silently and should NOT be implied
                       to be required.

  ADB over Wi-Fi:      No driver needed at all.

  Detection (current): config_loader._check_system_drivers() greps the
                       Windows driver store via `pnputil /enum-drivers`
                       for "android" / "adbinterface" / "androidusb" and
                       warns (does not block) on absence.
                       prerequisite_checker.scan_system() reports per-OEM
                       coverage to the UI without prompting an install.

  Recommended UX:      If `adb devices` returns empty AND the Google USB
                       Driver is absent, prompt the user to install it
                       (the only one that materially helps).  Do NOT
                       enumerate the OEM packages in the prompt — most
                       are irrelevant to a given user's device, and an
                       "unauthorized" line in `adb devices` is a trust-
                       prompt issue (not a driver issue) so a driver
                       install won't fix it.
